# Vectrix

> Zero-config time series forecasting for Python. 30+ models, auto selection, built-in Rust engine.

> Detailed version: https://eddmpython.github.io/vectrix/llms-full.txt

Vectrix is a Python library for time series forecasting. It evaluates 30+ statistical models (ETS, ARIMA, Theta, CES, DOT, MSTL, TBATS, GARCH, and more), validates each on a holdout set, and returns the best one with 95% confidence intervals — in a single function call.

## Key Features

- **Zero configuration**: Pass data, get forecasts. No model selection, no hyperparameter tuning.
- **30+ models**: Exponential smoothing, ARIMA, Theta, decomposition, intermittent demand, volatility, neural reservoir, pattern matching.
- **Adaptive intelligence**: Regime detection, self-healing forecasts, constraint enforcement, DNA-based meta-learning.
- **Business intelligence**: Anomaly detection, what-if scenarios, backtesting, business-specific accuracy metrics.
- **Built-in Rust engine**: 26 core hot loops Rust-accelerated (5-67x). Compiled into every wheel — like Polars.
- **Lightweight**: Only 3 dependencies (NumPy, pandas, SciPy).

## Installation

```
pip install vectrix
```

## Quick Start

```python
from vectrix import forecast
result = forecast([100, 120, 130, 115, 140, 160, 150, 170], steps=5)
print(result.model)        # Best model name
print(result.predictions)  # Forecast values
print(result.mape)         # Validation MAPE (%)
```

## Links

- Documentation: https://eddmpython.github.io/vectrix/docs/getting-started/installation
- API Reference: https://eddmpython.github.io/vectrix/docs/api/easy
- Tutorials: https://eddmpython.github.io/vectrix/docs/tutorials/quickstart
- Benchmarks: https://eddmpython.github.io/vectrix/docs/benchmarks
- GitHub: https://github.com/eddmpython/vectrix
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/vectrix/

## API Overview

### Easy API (one-line usage)

- `forecast(data, steps=10)` — Auto-select best model and forecast
- `analyze(data)` — DNA profiling with 65+ statistical features
- `regress(data, formula)` — R-style regression with diagnostics
- `compare(data, steps)` — Rank all 30+ models by accuracy

### Result Objects

- `EasyForecastResult`: `.predictions`, `.lower`, `.upper`, `.model`, `.mape`, `.rmse`, `.compare()`, `.to_dataframe()`, `.to_csv()`, `.to_json()`
- `EasyAnalysisResult`: `.dna` (fingerprint, difficulty, category, recommendedModels), `.characteristics`, `.changepoints`, `.anomalies`
- `EasyRegressionResult`: `.r_squared`, `.adj_r_squared`, `.f_stat`, `.summary()`, `.diagnose()`

### Vectrix Class (full control)

```python
from vectrix import Vectrix
vx = Vectrix()
result = vx.forecast(df, dateCol="date", valueCol="sales", steps=14, trainRatio=0.8)
```

### Engine Access (individual models)

```python
from vectrix.engine.ets import AutoETS
ets = AutoETS(period=12)
ets.fit(data)
predictions, lower, upper = ets.predict(steps=6)
```

### Business Intelligence

- `AnomalyDetector().detect(data, method="auto")` — Anomaly detection
- `WhatIfAnalyzer().analyze(predictions, data, scenarios)` — Scenario analysis
- `Backtester(nFolds=5).run(data, modelFactory)` — Walk-forward validation
- `BusinessMetrics().calculate(actual, predicted)` — Business accuracy metrics

### Adaptive Features

- `RegimeDetector(nRegimes=3).detect(data)` — HMM regime detection
- `SelfHealingForecast(preds, lower, upper, data)` — Real-time forecast correction
- `ConstraintAwareForecaster().apply(preds, lower, upper, constraints)` — Business constraint enforcement
- `ForecastDNA().analyze(data, period)` — 65+ feature extraction and meta-learning
